turns.user: Bah! Humbug! Christmas is a fraud. What reason have you to be merry? You're poor enough.
If I could work my will, every idiot going about with 'Merry Christmas' on his lips would be boiled with his own pudding and buried with a stake of holly through his heart.
Are there no prisons? Are there no workhouses? If the poor would rather die, let them do it and decrease the surplus population. It is not my business. My business is my business.
This ghost of Marley is humbug. There's more of gravy than of grave about you. I won't believe it.
Marley — you were always a good friend to me. Why are you fettered? Speak comfort to me.
I was happy once. As a boy, as an apprentice. When did I grow so hard? I chose gold over the girl I loved, and lost her.
That crippled child, Tiny Tim — will he live? I never once thought such people were any concern of mine.
My clerk's family is poor, and I have plenty, and I have given them nothing but a cold dark office. I begin to see what I am.
Whose grave is this — untended, unmourned, with no one sorry that he is gone? Tell me these are shadows of what may be, not what must be.
I am not the man I was. I will honour Christmas in my heart and try to keep it all the year. I can change. I will.
I'm still here! The shadows can be undone. I am light as a feather, happy as an angel, giddy as a drunken man.
I'll raise my clerk's salary today and help his struggling family. I'll be a second father to Tiny Tim. The old Scrooge is dead.
